source.description: Six-panel raster contact sheet generated for the May 26, 2026 honest archive backfill sketch lesson, reviewed before cropping.
source.notes: Prompt required one consistent vintage key with blank tag from construction to finished pencil sketch, with no embedded labels, numbers, arrows, signatures, or fake UI.

process_strategy: The sequence builds the key shaft, bow, teeth, blank tag, string loop, surface shadow, and restrained brass shading before the final panel only darkens keeper lines and clarifies existing details.

final_elements.name: long key shaft
final_elements.description: A straight horizontal key stem that anchors the drawing.

final_elements.name: round key bow
final_elements.description: A rounded handle loop at one end of the key.

final_elements.name: key teeth
final_elements.description: Small stepped cuts at the business end of the key.

final_elements.name: inner bow opening
final_elements.description: A smaller oval hole inside the key bow.

final_elements.name: blank paper tag
final_elements.description: A simple blank tag attached near the bow with no writing.

final_elements.name: string loop
final_elements.description: A short loop connecting the tag to the key.

final_elements.name: brass shading and surface shadow
final_elements.description: Restrained warm brass color on the key and a soft graphite shadow underneath.

frames.visible_job: Blocks the long shaft and round bow with light construction lines.
Adds stepped key teeth to the end of the existing shaft.
Adds the inner bow opening and cleans the main key silhouette.
Adds a blank paper tag and string loop near the bow.
Adds restrained brass shading to the existing key and a soft surface shadow.

final_step.visible_job: Darkens keeper contours, clarifies the bow, teeth, tag, and string loop, and deepens the existing brass shading and surface shadow.

rejection_checks: No shaft, bow, teeth, tag, string loop, shadow, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
